dataset.description: This dataset contains sales transaction data from Blinkit, an online grocery delivery platform. It contains sales data from Blinkit, including product details, order quantities, revenue, and timestamps.

desc: This dataset contains sales transaction data from Blinkit, an online grocery delivery platform. It contains sales data from Blinkit, including product details, order quantities, revenue, and timestamps.
goal: Analyze sales performance across various time dimensions—daily, weekly, and monthly—to identify peaks, troughs, and seasonal patterns. Visualizing historical sales trends helps in understanding how promotional events, holidays, or external factors affect sales, thereby providing valuable insights for inventory management and operational decision-making.
name: Visual Analytics System blinkit_goal1_1742643291

plan.path: The user browses View 1 at the Overview level and learns about the overall sales distribution across daily, weekly, and monthly time granularities. Next, the user brushes a specific time range in View 1, and observes that Views 2, 4, and 6 are updated accordingly. In View 2, the user explores daily and cumulative sales trends using the area chart. Then, the user drills down into holiday effects in View 3 and category-level sales contributions in View 4. Additionally, marketing influence on selected periods is analyzed in depth in View 6 through a scatter plot.

effect.description: The user brushes over a specific time range to filter data for subsequent views.

visualization.description: A multi-line chart showing total sales performance across daily, weekly, and monthly time granularities, enabling a comparison of trends.
encodings: x: blinkit_orders.order_date (time, aggregated as daily, weekly, and monthly)
color: time granularity (nominal, one for daily, weekly, monthly)

effect.description: The user brushes over a specific time interval to focus on detailed insights in subsequent views.

visualization.description: An area chart visualizing daily sales volumes, overlaid with a line chart showing cumulative monthly sales.
A line chart comparing sales during holidays versus regular days, with event periods highlighted.
encodings: color: event_period (nominal, holidays vs. regular days)

effect.description: The user clicks on a bar to drill down into detailed sales data for the selected category.

interactions.object: bars in the grouped bar chart

visualization.description: A grouped bar chart showing monthly sales by product category.
encodings: x: blinkit_products.category (nominal, grouped by product category)

visualization.description: A heatmap showing sales patterns across days of the week and hours of the day, with darker colors indicating higher sales volumes.
encodings: x: blinkit_orders.order_date (ordinal, day of the week)
y: blinkit_orders.order_time (ordinal, hour of the day)

effect.description: The user hovers over a specific campaign point to show campaign details and associated sales metrics.

visualization.description: A scatter plot correlating marketing spend with sales performance, with campaigns differentiated by color and size depicting sales volume.

data.reason: This allows us to analyze sales patterns and their fluctuations over different time frames, which aligns with the user's goal of identifying seasonal trends.
data.requirement: Extract and aggregate sales data (order_total) from the 'blinkit_orders' table across three time dimensions: daily, weekly, and monthly.

data.reason: Overlaying sales data with marketing campaign timelines helps reveal the effectiveness of promotions in driving sales performance.
data.requirement: Integrate promotional data (campaign_name and spend) from the 'blinkit_marketing_performance' table with sales data to analyze how campaigns influence sales peaks and troughs.

data.reason: Understanding the impact of holidays and events on sales aids in identifying external factors driving peaks or troughs in sales patterns.
data.requirement: Identify and extract holiday or event-based sales data by mapping sales records (order_date) to external event calendars.

data.reason: Derived metrics help go beyond simple aggregation to identify variability and growth trends in sales performance.
data.requirement: Compute derived metrics such as average daily sales, standard deviation, and cumulative monthly sales from the aggregated data for deeper insights into sales performance.

data.reason: Adding category-level granularity allows for a more actionable understanding of which product categories contribute most to seasonal trends.
data.requirement: Include the 'category' feature from the 'blinkit_products' table to break down sales performance by product category.

interaction.reason: Time-based filtering enables users to focus on specific periods to analyze sales trends more closely.
interaction.requirement: Allow users to filter sales data by specific time ranges (e.g., months or weeks) using a brush or slider selection.

interaction.reason: Hover provides micro-level insights without overwhelming the visualization, helping users quickly access relevant data points.
interaction.requirement: Integrate hover interactions to display detailed metrics (e.g., daily sales total, number of orders) on line or area charts.

interaction.reason: This interaction ensures users can seamlessly switch between various temporal dimensions for flexible analysis.
interaction.requirement: Enable users to toggle between different time aggregations (daily, weekly, monthly) directly in the line chart view.

interaction.reason: Filtering campaigns helps users isolate their impact, determining which campaigns were most effective for driving sales growth.
interaction.requirement: In the scatter plot, allow users to highlight and filter specific marketing campaigns (campaign_name) to see their contributions to sales trends.

interaction.reason: Drill-down interactions allow for detailed exploration of aggregated data, helping users identify drivers behind observed sales patterns.
interaction.requirement: Provide drill-down interactions on bar charts or heat maps to view category-specific sales data or sales patterns within specific weeks.

visualization.reason: Line charts are optimal for visualizing continuous trends over time and enable easy detection of peaks and troughs.
visualization.requirement: Use a line chart to visualize sales trends over time (daily, weekly, monthly) for overall sales performance.

visualization.reason: This dual visualization allows users to understand both granular and aggregated sales trends in a single view.
visualization.requirement: Combine area charts with line charts to show cumulative monthly sales alongside daily sales trends.

visualization.reason: Bar charts are effective for comparing categorical data, highlighting how product categories contribute to sales over time.
visualization.requirement: Generate a bar chart to compare monthly sales across different product categories derived from the 'category' column.

visualization.reason: Heat maps allow for an intuitive representation of dense data across two categorical dimensions, facilitating the detection of spikes or anomalies in sales activity.
visualization.requirement: Utilize a heat map to show weekly sales patterns across days of the week and hours of the day.

visualization.reason: Scatter plots are effective for identifying relationships between two numerical variables, such as spend and resulting sales.
visualization.requirement: Create a scatter plot to correlate advertising spend (spend) with sales performance, highlighting campaigns from the 'blinkit_marketing_performance' table.

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW dailySales AS SELECT T1.order_date, SUM(T1.order_total) AS total_order_amount FROM blinkit_orders AS T1 WHERE T1.order_date >= '2020-01-01' AND T1.order_date <= '2023-12-31' GROUP BY T1.order_date
sqls.name: dailySales

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW weeklySales AS SELECT DATE_TRUNC('week', T1.order_date) AS week_start_date, SUM(T1.order_total) AS total_order_amount FROM blinkit_orders AS T1 WHERE T1.order_date >= '2020-01-01' AND T1.order_date <= '2023-12-31' GROUP BY week_start_date
sqls.name: weeklySales

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW monthlySales AS SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', T1.order_date) AS month_start_date, SUM(T1.order_total) AS total_order_amount FROM blinkit_orders AS T1 WHERE T1.order_date >= '2020-01-01' AND T1.order_date <= '2023-12-31' GROUP BY month_start_date
sqls.name: monthlySales

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW detailedDailySales AS SELECT T1.order_date, SUM(T1.order_total) AS total_order_amount FROM blinkit_orders AS T1 WHERE T1.order_date >= '2020-01-01' AND T1.order_date <= '2023-12-31' GROUP BY T1.order_date
sqls.name: detailedDailySales

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW holidaySales AS SELECT T1.order_date, SUM(T1.order_total) AS total_order_amount, CASE WHEN T2.holiday IS NULL THEN 'Regular' ELSE 'Holiday' END AS event_period FROM blinkit_orders AS T1 LEFT JOIN external_event_calendar AS T2 ON DATE(T1.order_date) = DATE(T2.date) WHERE T1.order_date >= '2020-01-01' AND T1.order_date <= '2023-12-31' GROUP BY T1.order_date, CASE WHEN T2.holiday IS NULL THEN 'Regular' ELSE 'Holiday' END
sqls.name: holidaySales

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW categorySales AS SELECT T2.category, SUM(T1.order_total) AS total_order_amount FROM blinkit_orders AS T1 JOIN blinkit_order_items AS T3 ON T1.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN blinkit_products AS T2 ON T3.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T1.order_date >= '2020-01-01' AND T1.order_date <= '2023-12-31' GROUP BY T2.category
sqls.name: categorySales

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW heatmapData AS SELECT TO_CHAR(T1.order_date, 'D') AS day_of_week, EXTRACT(HOUR FROM T1.order_date) AS hour_of_day, SUM(T1.order_total) AS total_order_amount FROM blinkit_orders AS T1 WHERE T1.order_date >= '2020-01-01' AND T1.order_date <= '2023-12-31' GROUP BY TO_CHAR(T1.order_date, 'D'), EXTRACT(HOUR FROM T1.order_date)
sqls.name: heatmapData

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW campaignSales AS SELECT T2.campaign_name, SUM(T1.order_total) AS total_order_amount, T1.order_date FROM blinkit_orders AS T1 JOIN blinkit_marketing_performance AS T2 ON T1.campaign_id = T2.campaign_id WHERE T1.order_date >= '2020-01-01' AND T1.order_date <= '2023-12-31' GROUP BY T2.campaign_name, T1.order_date
sqls.name: campaignSales

tasks.method: Aggregate sales data by computing daily, weekly, and monthly sales totals and averages. Visualize these using a multi-line chart (V1), with one line for each time granularity to compare trends.
tasks.objective: Understand overall sales performance across different time dimensions, highlighting peaks, troughs, and seasonal patterns.

tasks.method: Correlate marketing campaign expenditure (spend) with daily sales performance using a scatter plot (V5). Campaigns can be highlighted and filtered for deeper insights into performance during specific time periods.
tasks.objective: Analyze how marketing efforts (spend) influence sales trends, helping optimize future campaigns for better ROI.

tasks.method: Overlay sales data with an external event calendar. Highlight holiday periods on a line chart (V1) to compare sales fluctuations during holidays versus regular days.
tasks.objective: Identify the impact of holidays and events on sales, helping plan inventory and marketing strategies during peak periods.

tasks.method: Calculate derived metrics (average daily sales, standard deviation, cumulative monthly sales) and create an area chart overlaid with a line chart (V2) to show cumulative sales alongside daily patterns.
tasks.objective: Gain deeper insights into sales variability and growth trends over time.

tasks.method: Break down sales data by monthly sales totals across product categories using a grouped bar chart (V3). Allow drill-down interactions to explore individual categories in greater detail.
tasks.objective: Analyze category-level contributions to overall sales trends, helping identify high-performing product categories.

tasks.method: Use a heat map (V4) to visualize sales patterns across days of the week and hours of the day, with intensity representing sales volume. Enable drill-down interactions to view sales within specific weeks or time slots.
tasks.objective: Identify spikes or anomalies in sales activity across time intervals, enabling better resource allocation and scheduling.

tasks.method: Compute average monthly sales per campaign for each product category. Use a stacked bar chart to visualize overlapping contributions of campaigns to category-wise sales.
tasks.objective: Understand how specific campaigns influence sales by category and help tailor marketing strategies for targeted segments.
